policy_scope.purpose: Govern differential-oracle placement, retention, quarantine, expiry, and retirement for every dependency-sovereignty replacement campaign.
policy_scope.current_incumbent_rule: A package that is still an incumbent production edge is not yet a retained oracle. Post-cutover oracle rules become mandatory before the production edge is removed.
policy_scope.cutover_rule: No dependency may move from incumbent production use to retained-oracle use until this registry row is activated with concrete release and expiry values and all lane checks pass.

profile_definitions.meaning: Normal production dependency graph of an asupersync consumer.
Asupersync workspace dev-dependency and ordinary test graph.
Host build-script and proc-macro graph used by ordinary checks, tests, or releases.
Published or packaged release graph and its release verification lanes.
Explicit fuzz-only graph excluded from production and ordinary workspace claims.
Separate Cargo project not included in the asupersync workspace or release graph.
Oracle execution owned by a downstream repository that already depends on asupersync.
Cycle-safe external consumer that depends on both systems under comparison.
Immutable vectors or transcripts consumed without resolving or building the incumbent package.

oracle_classes.description: A bounded pure-Rust reference may remain in workspace dev or fuzz lanes when feature unification cannot contaminate production and the retention window is no more than two releases.
An oracle with active native, C, C++, assembly, configure/make, bundled-library, or prohibited host-build exposure must leave every ordinary asupersync workspace graph after cutover.
A downstream project that already depends on asupersync must own the comparison, or the comparison must run in a neutral synthesized consumer; it may never return as an asupersync dev dependency.

A protocol, credential, telemetry, or security parser reference is quarantined and must carry corpus provenance, strict secret redaction, resource bounds, and either bounded expiry or permanent-KEEP owner justification.

oracle_registry.no_claim_boundary: Agreement with hex does not prove correctness outside the exercised corpus.
Reference agreement does not establish constant-time behavior or credential secrecy.
Differential completion values do not prove cancellation safety, fairness, or quiescence.
Index parity does not prove leak freedom or stale-handle safety.
Matching macro expansion does not prove identical compiler diagnostics across toolchains.
Round-trip agreement does not prove historical migration completeness.
Byte agreement does not prove every downstream Serde data model is supported.
Reference parsing does not authorize dropping accepted TOML syntax or comments.
Reference parsing does not authorize silent YAML data loss or unsupported syntax.
CLI snapshot agreement does not prove shell integration or installed-package usability.
Match parity does not prove linear-time behavior, bounded memory, or privacy completeness.

oracle_registry.secret_redaction: required; only synthetic seeds may enter fixtures and logs
oracle_registry.no_claim_boundary: Agreement with nkeys does not prove signer authorization, secret zeroization, or cryptographic soundness.
Wire agreement does not prove arbitrary downstream schema ergonomics or resource safety.
Reference agreement does not prove clock capability correctness or every civil-time rule.
Behavioral agreement does not prove fairness, liveness, or performance parity.
Decompression agreement does not prove resource bounds or historical replay completeness.
Codec agreement does not prove bounded memory, compression quality, or all producer compatibility.
Host metric agreement does not prove accuracy on unexercised kernels or machines.
Agreement between parsers does not prove certificate-path validation or resistance to all malicious DER.
Generated-message agreement does not prove collector interoperability or production no-Tokio graphs.
External agreement does not certify librdkafka, brokers, or the owned client as memory-safe or protocol-complete.
Parity does not certify SQLite C code or prove every extension and durability mode.
AST agreement does not prove authorization, query safety, or dialect completeness.
Dual-engine agreement does not prove durability, cancellation, or all SQLite semantics beyond the matrix.

validation.proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${RCH_TARGET_BASE:-${TMPDIR:-/tmp}}/rch_target_dependency_oracle_policy" cargo test -p asupersync --test dependency_oracle_policy_contract -- --nocapture
explicit_no_claims: This policy does not prove either implementation is correct.
This policy does not prove native code is safe or absent before a conditional production cutover.
This policy does not prove broad workspace health, release readiness, performance parity, or interoperability.
Planned harness URIs are ownership contracts, not evidence that the harness already exists.
